title: MANGA NGE Volume 11
wikitext:
[]: [MANGA] [NGE] Volume 11             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 16, 2007 12:36 pm I've been doing some fuzzy math, trying to figure out whether or not volume 11 is finished. The volumes run about 170 pages each, with 7 stages a piece. I went through my scans of stages 72-77, which came up to 137 pages. I'm missing scans of stage 71, but Bochan Bird's comments for his translation of that stage states that 71 was a long stage. Depending on how long it was, volume 11 could be complete. Doing some more fuzzy math, the last three volumes of the Eva manga were published within 4 months of the last stage in the issue. Which means that the Japanese volume 11 could possibly be out in May. Could someone with scans of Stage 71 do a page count so I can get a more accurate page count?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Apr 17, 2007 3:00 pm 71 has 27 pages, but I believe one of them is a double one.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Apr 17, 2007 4:30 pm Thanks. Tack that onto the four initial pages of artwork, and we have at least 168 pages towards the new volume, which should be enough for them to publish a volume. Unless they're holding off because Sadamoto plans to publish a ridiculously short stage. Whatever the case, we should know within the next month.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Apr 17, 2007 7:17 pm I don't understand how the Manga can be puclished in stages in Japan when its releases are so sporadic. Is it part of a larger publication? If so, how come no one has had the idea od bundling various Japanese mangas into a large english translated volume?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Apr 17, 2007 7:28 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I don't understand how the Manga can be puclished in stages in Japan when its releases are so sporadic. Is it part of a larger publication? If so, how come no one has had the idea od bundling various Japanese mangas into a large english translated volume?

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Tue Apr 17, 2007 7:41 pm volume 11 will be given to us in March of 2012...thats my prediction...

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Tue Apr 17, 2007 8:09 pm Well then, I've got 5 years to save up $13. I think I can make it.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Apr 18, 2007 3:17 pm Even less actually. They usually sell for $9.99

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Wed Apr 18, 2007 4:31 pm usually I get my books from amazon and they charge like 3-4 bucks for standard shipping which is annoying to the point of head explosion. Besides, theres a better chance of me becoming Mr. Universe than the chance that my local B&N would actually have it in stock.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Wed Apr 18, 2007 5:04 pm really?...my Barnes and Noble has a massive manga section...theyve got almost every Hot to Draw Manga book and they have all of the Eva Manga...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 18, 2007 5:43 pm Tokpile Quohog wrote: usually I get my books from amazon and they charge like 3-4 bucks for standard shipping which is annoying to the point of head explosion. Besides, theres a better chance of me becoming Mr. Universe than the chance that my local B&N would actually have it in stock.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Thu Apr 19, 2007 3:46 pm So just go with the free shipping deal. So what if it take an extra day or two. I usually buy something from amazon every month or two and I have never payed any shipping fee. Edit: I just looked, Evangelion #10 is Eligible for FREE Super Saver Shipping.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Thu Apr 19, 2007 3:54 pm amazon's free super saver shipping are only eligible for orders over $25. So first the product has to be approved for free shipping then your order has to be $25 or more and I normally don't buy $25 worth of things there.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Thu Apr 19, 2007 4:09 pm Then just hold off buying till you do. The only time I order from amazon is when there are a couple things I want that way I don't have to pay as much.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Thu Apr 19, 2007 6:46 pm But the only thing I buy on amazon is the manga :( .

[]:             Quote Post by Ejinathan » Fri Apr 27, 2007 3:50 am Tokpile Quohog wrote: But the only thing I buy on amazon is the manga :( .

[]:             Quote Post by HenkZw » Fri Apr 27, 2007 5:38 am Ejinathan wrote: Tokpile Quohog wrote: But the only thing I buy on amazon is the manga :( .

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Apr 27, 2007 9:02 am damn i've been hearing all sorts of good things about eureka 7. I'm assuming the Japanese cast is fairly good? I saw the first couple episodes on Adult Swim last year and thought it was the stupidest thing ever. I'm hoping its just the poor quality english dub. sorry for off topic-ness

[]:             Quote Post by HenkZw » Sun Apr 29, 2007 4:53 pm Also sorry for off-topicness. I liked the voices - and the music was also quite good. I didn't like the first episodes; which I watched on Youtube. But at episode 11, I started saving the episodes. I have now ordered most of the DVDs out now. And the music CDs. Eureka Seven has a happier ending than Evangelion. But it's not as deep.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Sun Apr 29, 2007 5:47 pm Utilizing skills of "getting back on topic", any word on volume 11?
